title: The AI Interface Layer Will Pick Winners and Losers for a Generation

source.original: 'Nothing Ever Happens' Is Over: The Fully Interconnected Startup — Naval Podcast

body_html:
Naval identifies AI interfaces as the next critical inflection point — not the models themselves, but how humans interact with them. This distinction matters enormously. The raw capability of foundation models is increasingly commoditized; what isn't commoditized is the design of the interaction layer that determines whether a user gets 10x leverage or gets overwhelmed by noise.

Think of it as the browser moment for AI. In the early web, the underlying protocols were open and available to everyone. But the companies that built the best interfaces — the browsers, the search engines, the social platforms — captured the lion's share of value. AI is following the same pattern. The model is the protocol; the interface is the product.

This has profound implications for startups. Competing on model quality against well-funded labs is a losing proposition for most teams. But competing on interface design, workflow integration, and domain-specific UX is wide open. The teams that understand how knowledge workers, scientists, or operators actually think and work — and build AI interactions around those patterns — will define the next generation of software.

There's a darker dimension here too. AI interfaces also determine who gets left behind. If the primary way to harness AI requires technical fluency or specific cultural context, the gap between AI-literate and AI-illiterate populations will widen dramatically. The interface layer isn't just a business opportunity — it's a power structure.

The race to define how humanity relates to AI systems is happening now, largely in the open, and largely without regulatory guidance. Whoever sets the patterns — whether through consumer products, enterprise tools, or operating system integrations — will shape work, education, and power distribution for at least a decade. This is not a sideshow. It's the main event.
